name: Hyundai Motor India Ltd.
ai:
I'll check the fundamentals from screener.in and then search for recent news about Hyundai Motor India. Let me search for more recent news from the last 3 weeks specifically. CATALYST:  April 2026 domestic sales hit 51,902 units, highest monthly April tally ever ; followed by product pipeline (new Bayon SUV July-September 2026).

THEME: India auto sector momentum; peers Maruti (MARUTI), Tata Motors (TATAMOTORS) also pushing SUV-heavy launches.

FUNDAMENTALS:  Strong ROE of 36.8% over 3 years ;  company almost debt-free ; but  poor 11.2% sales growth over 5 years .

VERDICT: Mixed — strong recent sales offset by structurally slow growth trend.

CALL: WAIT — growth inflection unproven, valuation fair but not compelling.

WHY_CALL: April sales surge is promising and new SUV launches could drive near-term volumes, but 5-year CAGR of 11% shows structural headwinds in competitive Indian auto market. At 22x P/E and ROE of 37%, valuation is reasonable for the quality but offers limited margin of safety on a recent 7% outperformance. Key inflection would be sustained double-digit domestic growth and positive earnings revisions on new model ramp; watch Q1 FY27 results and new Bayon traction closely before adding.

name: AARTIDRUGS
ai:
I'll check the screener data and recent news for Aarti Drugs. Let me search for more recent news from the past 3 weeks. Based on the data gathered, here's the analysis in your requested format:

CATALYST:  Q1 FY27 earnings report on July 23, 2026 .
THEME: Pharma API smallcap recovery; stock-specific turnaround attempt.
FUNDAMENTALS:  Sales grew just 3.55% over five years; profit flat at 195 crore ; recent Q4 saw  net profit fell 12.2% .
VERDICT: Mixed — weak earnings, margin pressure, operational recovery uncertain.
CALL: WAIT — valuation stretched after 3% run despite deteriorating fundamentals.
WHY_CALL:  Stock showed recovery after restoring Tarapur unit production , but  ROCE has fallen from 21.5% to 14.6% over three years .  Promoter holding fell to 54.43% from 55.7%  (minor concern). Earnings imminent July 23—better to see results before entering at near-52-week highs. Risk/reward poor until Q1 proves margin recovery genuine.

WHY_CALL:  Accelerating profits (40% growth) and sales (25% growth in December quarter)  backed by real market recognition. <cite index="1-8">Stock trades at somewhat overvalued zone by historical measures</cite>; recent 28% run needs more earnings quarters to justify fully. Recent recognition boosts investor confidence but entry not at rock-bottom—add 1/3 now, hold for next quarter result.

name: Tech Mahindra Ltd.
ai:
I'll check the fundamentals and search for recent news on Tech Mahindra. Let me search for more recent developments and promoter holding changes. CATALYST:  Q1 FY27 revenue beat (₹15,712 cr, +17.7% YoY) announced July 16, 2026 
THEME: IT services sector turnaround; peers TCS, HCL showing similar strength
FUNDAMENTALS:  Deal wins $1.08B (+33% YoY), 11 consecutive quarters of margin expansion to 14.4% 
VERDICT: Hard news — strong earnings, margins, deal velocity

WHY_CALL:  Three consecutive quarters of $1B+ deal wins underscores business resilience; all verticals growing YoY with $50M+ client base expanding  But  P/E of 36.65  and  5-year sales CAGR only 8.46%  mean valuation is not cheap relative to modest core-business growth.  Demand uncertainty and premium valuations warrant balanced approach . Risk: macroeconomic slowdown could dent visibility. Would shift to BUY-NOW if margins sustain 15%+ and revenue growth accelerates to double-digits consistently.

name: Relaxo Footwears Ltd.
ai:
I'll check the fundamentals from screener.in and search for recent news on Relaxo Footwears. CATALYST:  Retail format refresh unveiled July 10, 2026 ; stock surged 13.78% on July 17 on 15x volume.
THEME: Consumer durables / footwear; stock-specific rebranding, no peer catalyst evident.
FUNDAMENTALS: Purely price action.  Operating profit contracted -10.87% over 5 years ,  sales grew only 2.75% in 5 years ,  ROE 8.1% .
VERDICT: Speculative — volume spike without earnings/order data to back run.
CALL: AVOID — deteriorating profitability, weak execution history conflicts with recent move.
WHY_CALL: The July 10 store relaunch is cosmetic; no material revenue/margin catalyst. Fundamentals show 5-year profit decline and revenue stagnation despite earlier restructuring. The volume spike and breakout look like retail euphoria on optics alone, not a real turnaround. Risk: this move unwinds fast if volumes and orders don't improve visibly in next quarter's results.

Wait for a pullback or near-term results disappointment for a cleaner WAIT-to-BUY entry for a long-term starter position.

Board approved ₹1,000 crore NCD fundraise in June to bolster capital adequacy . Main risk:  Low interest coverage ratio and stock already up 40% in one year . Catalyst is just investor communication, not earnings surprise—would need Q1 execution clarity to add more confidence.

CATALYST:  Q1 FY27 results expected July-August 2026, AGM on 16 July 2026  with  dividend ex-date 9 July 2026 .
THEME: Mattress industry consolidation; post-Kurlon integration growth story with mattress and e-commerce tailwinds vs peers like Sheela, Furlenco.
FUNDAMENTALS:  Q4 FY26 PAT jumped 707% to Rs 92 crore, revenue up 23.5% YoY ;  mattress volumes up 11% YoY in Q3 .
VERDICT: Mixed —  weak 3-year ROE of 3.86%, poor 5-year sales growth , recent earnings recovery.
CALL: WAIT —  P/E 55.3, ROE only 4.80%  despite strong recent results, needs larger quarters.
WHY_CALL: Kurlon deal drove recent earnings spike but returns remain poor relative to valuation. Strong Q4 recovery is real but one quarter doesn't validate high multiples. Wait for Q1 FY27 earnings to confirm sustainability and see if management upgrades medium-term growth guidance beyond 15%.

THEME:  India's defence sector: rising capex, indigenisation, Middle East tensions ; peer tickers BEL, BDL.
